mox-sea.body:
Hi there, hope all is well at the Mox.

Would you give MeepleUp a try with your open gaming crowd? It's a free app I built for groups that game together regularly — members RSVP between sessions, nominate games from a shared library, and vote on each other's picks — so they show up knowing what they're playing instead of working it out when they sit down. If Mox Boarding House Seattle is on BoardGameGeek the library syncs in one shot — or AI photo recognition can read the shelves directly.

I've spent over a thousand hours on this and I'm in early release, so there are almost certainly bugs I haven't found yet. If anything feels off, please email contact@meepleup.com before leaving a review — I'd much rather fix it. If it works well, a rating on the App Store or Google Play would mean a lot.

meepleup.com or search "MeepleUp" on the App Store / Google Play.

Thanks,
Rhys Smoker

I read about how Owlbear's Rest came out of a long car ride and a question about what really matters — and that coffee, games, and community were the answer. The groups that come back to play together are exactly who I built MeepleUp for.

It's a free app for groups that game together regularly. Between visits, members RSVP, nominate games from a shared library, and vote — so they arrive knowing what they're playing instead of figuring it out after they sit down. Getting the library in is easy — AI reads games from shelf photos, or syncs from BoardGameGeek if Owlbear's Rest is already listed there.

Would you give it a try? I'm in early release and still finding bugs — if anything doesn't work right, please email contact@meepleup.com before leaving a review. I'd rather fix it. If it all works, a rating on the App Store or Google Play would mean a lot.

meepleup.com or search "MeepleUp" on the App Store / Google Play.

Thanks,
Rhys Smoker
